description: In 2021, Chile passed a landmark law prohibiting food establishments from using single-use plastics like cutlery, straws, and polystyrene containers. The law was championed by environmental NGOs like Oceana and eventually found broad legislative consensus despite resistance from the restaurant and catering industry.

key_events.description: Bill moves to the Senate after strong advocacy from Oceana Chile.
National Restaurant Association warns of increased costs for small businesses.
Government compromises on a phase-in period for small businesses to gain support.
Congress approves the final text of the bill with overwhelming majority.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how consensus shifts during the legislative process when stakeholders successfully negotiate transition periods.
